title: Early triage at the top of the router, so a tiny task never loads the planning skill
action: Per D7. Add a compact triage block immediately after the `# hive` intro in skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md, BEFORE the Onboarding section. Its single job: let a session decide docs / tiny / small / everything-else from the request alone and act, without loading skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md (21 KB). It must state, in a few lines: the risk-flag count and product-file count that separate the lanes; that docs / tiny / small route straight to the merged shape+execution gate and the one dispatched execution worker, with no planning-skill load; and that standard / high-risk (or any hard-gate flag, or genuine uncertainty) fall through to the normal chain, which does load bee-planning. Uncertainty resolves DOWNWARD into loading more, never upward into skipping: the block is an early exit for the obviously-small, never a licence to shortcut a hard-gate change. Keep it short — a block that restates the whole Modes and Lanes section defeats its own purpose. DO NOT touch the pinned Modes and Lanes region: all ten pins in D6 must survive character-for-character, and this cell adds text rather than rewording any of it. Then the D8 regen chain: `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write` (skills/** is hashed at release_manifest.mjs:131) plus a self-onboard pass so the four rendered skill roots carry the new text.

truths: All seven REQUIRED pins from D6 are still present verbatim in skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md.
All three BANNED strings from D6 are still absent.
The triage block appears before the Onboarding section and names the docs/tiny/small early exit explicitly.
The block states that uncertainty falls through to the full chain rather than shortcutting it.
The four rendered skill roots carry the new text and release_manifest --check is green.
artifacts: The triage block in skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md, refreshed renders and manifest
prohibitions: Never reword anything inside the pinned Modes and Lanes region in this cell.
Never let the triage block authorise skipping the chain for a hard-gate flag.

verify: node scripts/test_gate_bypass_doctrine.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_misc.mjs && node scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs && node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Added a 15-line additive 'Triage first' block to skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md, sitting after the intro pointer paragraph and before ## Onboarding. It decides docs/tiny/small/standard from the request alone via the two mechanical counts (risk flags, product files) and routes the first three straight to the merged shape+execution gate and the one dispatched execution worker with no bee-planning load. Purely additive: the pinned ## Modes and Lanes region is byte-untouched, and all ten pins verified by direct grep. The block states D7's honesty in its own text — that it saves nothing on bee-hive itself because skills load whole-file, and the only saving on offer is the second load — so a later reader cannot mistake it for a short-circuit on reading the router. It closes the two escape routes a model under context pressure would reach for: one hard-gate flag is high-risk at one product file, and re-counting flags to land under a threshold is itself the tell that you are already in standard. Uncertainty resolves downward into loading more, never upward into skipping.
